title: NPTEL IoT 2024 JULY

questions.question: IoT stands for ____________.
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of IoT is "Internet of Things"
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 1:30

questions.question: Which of the following technologies have unified and has resulted in the evolution of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Unification of technologies which has resulted in the advancement of
IoT are -
a. Low-power embedded systems
b. Cloud Computing
c. Big Data
d. Machine Learning
e. Networking
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 5:54

questions.question: Which of the following are the enables of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The enables of IoT are -
a. RFID
b. Nanotechnology
c. Sensors
d. Smart Networks
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 12:50

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT Gateway?

options.text: Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN
Can implement several LAN and WAN

questions.detailed_solution:
The functionalities of an IoT Gateway are -
a. Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN
b. Can implement several LAN and WAN
c. Forwards packets between LAN and WAN on the IP layer
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 3:09

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: "The ITU vision is approaching reality as the present-day networked devices have outnumbered humans on earth."
questions.detailed_solution:
The ITU vision is approaching reality as the present-day networked
devices have outnumbered humans on earth.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 02:35

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "In Multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability.
questions.detailed_solution:
In multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for
improved reliability.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 15:22

questions.question: Which of the following are the features of an IoT Gateway?

options.text: A router connecting IoT LAN and WAN to Internet
Can implement several LAN and WAN
Forwards packets between LAN and WAN

questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the features of an IoT gateway -
 A router connecting IoT LAN and WAN to the Internet
 Can implement several LAN and WAN
 Forwards packets between LAN and WAN
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 04:00

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "IoT Proxy performs active application layer functions between IoT nodes and other entities."
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT Proxy performs active application layer functions between IoT
nodes and other entities.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 04:00

questions.question: The Gateway has a ____________ address prefix, which can be used to identify them globally.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Gateway has a unique address prefix, which can be used to identify
them globally.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 07:00

questions.question: The IPv4 notation uses ___________________ number of bits to represent an address.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv4 notation uses 32 bits to represent an address.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 16:33

questions.question: The "Destination Address" field in the IPv4 header is of __________ bits in length.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv4 uses 32 bits to represent the "Destination Address".
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 17:53

questions.question: We can sense temperature using _____________?
questions.detailed_solution: We can sense temperature using a Sensor.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "A sensor is not only sensitive to the measured property, but it also influences the measured property."
questions.detailed_solution:
A sensor is only sensitive to the measured property. It does not
influence the measured property.
.

questions.question: Based on the output, sensors are classified as _______________________.
questions.detailed_solution: Based on the output, sensors are classified as Digital and Analog.

questions.question: What is the full form of SMP in the context of IoT actuators?
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of SMP is Shape Memory Polymers.
questions.reference: See Page number - 111, Chapter - 5, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: Based on functionality, MQTT is a _______ protocol.
questions.detailed_solution: MQTT is a Data Protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:30

questions.question: MQTT is a ___________-based lightweight protocol for use in conjunction with TCP/IP protocol.
questions.detailed_solution:
MQTT, is a publish-subscribe-based lightweight protocol for use in
conjunction with TCP/IP protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 02:12

questions.question: A ____________ controls the publish-subscribe messaging pattern in MQTT.
questions.detailed_solution: A Broker controls the publish-subscribe messaging pattern in MQTT.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 03:31

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "In MQTT, the Publishers are Lightweight Sensors."
questions.detailed_solution: In MQTT, the Publishers are lightweight sensors.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 04:49

questions.question: Which of the following is MQTT designed for?
questions.detailed_solution:
MQTT is designed for -
a. Remote connections
b. Limited bandwidth
c. Small code footprint
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 03:23

questions.question: A __________ to which a client is subscribed is updated in the form of messages and distributed by the ____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
A topic to which a client is subscribed is updated in the form of
messages and distributed by the message broker.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 04:00

questions.question: There are _______ number of methods in MQTT protocol.
questions.detailed_solution: There are 5 number of methods in MQTT protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 05:49

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Publish/Subscribe in MQTT is event-driven and enables messages to be pushed to clients."
questions.detailed_solution:
Publish/Subscribe in MQTT is event-driven and enables messages to be
pushed to clients.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 08:32

questions.question: SMQTT stands for?
questions.detailed_solution: SMQTT stands for Secure MQTT.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 14:10

questions.question: Similar to HTTP, CoAP utilizes which of the following for operation?
questions.detailed_solution:
Similar to HTTP, CoAP utilizes GET, PUT, PUSH, DELETE message
requests to retrieve, create, update, and delete, respectively.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 08:24

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "The CoAP protocol is a Client-Server interaction-based protocol that is asynchronous over a datagram-oriented transport protocol such as UDP."
questions.detailed_solution:
The CoAP protocol is a Client-Server interaction-based protocol that is
asynchronous over a datagram-oriented transport protocol such as UDP.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 00:30

questions.question: What is the full form of M2M in the context of IoT protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: M2M stands for Machine to Machine.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 0:30

questions.question: What is the basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: In AMQP, the basic unit of data is called a frame.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 0:41

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "The OSI model has 9 layers."
questions.detailed_solution:
The OSI model is a conceptual framework that divides any networked
communication system into seven layers.
questions.reference: See Page number - 10, Chapter - 1, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents which of the following?

options.text: The destination node address of the packet
The intermediate hop in the network

text: The destination node address of the packet

questions.detailed_solution:
The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents the address of
the destination node in the network.
questions.reference: See Page number - 18, Chapter - 1, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)
questions.answer_display: The destination node address of the packet

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Wireless HART is the latest release of Highway Addressable Remote Transducer protocol."
questions.detailed_solution:
WirelessHART is the latest release of Highway Addressable Remote
Transducer protocol.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Wired HART does not have a network layer."
questions.detailed_solution: Wired HART does not have a network layer.

questions.question: Wireless HART physical layer is derived from ______________ protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: WirelessHART physical layer is derived from 802.15.4 protocol.

questions.question: Wireless HART operates only in __________ GHz ISM band.
questions.detailed_solution: HART operates only in the 2.4 GHz ISM band.

questions.question: Wireless HART employs and exploits __________ channels of the ISM band to increase reliability.
questions.detailed_solution:
WirelessHART employs and exploits 15 channels of the ISM band to
increase reliability.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Channel blacklisting identifies channels consistently affected by interference and adds them from use again."
questions.detailed_solution:
Channel blacklisting identifies channels consistently affected by
interference and removes them from use.

questions.question: The Wireless HART incorporates ____________ and ____________ to increase the reliability and security of the network.
questions.detailed_solution:
The WirelessHART incorporates Channel hopping and channel
blacklisting to increase the reliability and security of the network.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False Statement: "Wireless HART protocol relies on Mesh Networking for its communication, and each device is primed to forward packets from every other device.".
questions.detailed_solution:
WirelessHART protocol relies on Mesh Networking for its
communication, and each device is primed to forward packets from every other device.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "ZigBee protocol does not feature channel hopping at all."
questions.detailed_solution: ZigBee protocol does not feature channel hopping at all.

questions.question: NFC is designed for use by devices within ________________ to each other.
questions.detailed_solution:
NFC is designed for use by devices within close proximity to each
other.

questions.question: NFC are of ________ types?
questions.detailed_solution: NFC are of 2 types.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Smartphones are an example of active NFC devices."
questions.detailed_solution: Smartphones are an example of active NFC devices.

questions.question: Bluetooth technology is based on _________________.
questions.detailed_solution: Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc piconets.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment, authentication, link configuration.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment,
authentication, link configuration.
questions.reference: See Page number - 157, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: Zigbee commonly uses __________data rate.
questions.detailed_solution: Zigbee commonly uses a 250 kbps data rate.
questions.reference: See Page number - 131, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Coverage in WSN is defined as the entire area is covered satisfactorily.
questions.detailed_solution:
Coverage in WSN is defined as the area-of-interest is covered
satisfactorily.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "In Wireless Sensor Network coverage, we study how to deploy or activate sensors to cover the monitoring area."
questions.detailed_solution:
In Wireless Sensor Network coverage, we study how to deploy or
activate sensors to cover the monitoring area.

questions.question: Water reservoir overflow monitoring in WSN is an example of which type of reporting?
questions.detailed_solution:
Water reservoir overflow monitoring in WSN is an example of event
driven reporting.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Objective of coverage in WSN is to use minimum number of sensors and minimize network lifetime.
questions.detailed_solution:
The objective of coverage in WSN is to use minimum number of
sensors and maximize the network lifetime.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: "Sensor nodes in WSN can be of two types - 1. Static sensor nodes, and 2. Mobile sensor nodes."
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor nodes in WSN can be of only two types - 1. Static sensor nodes,
and 2. Mobile sensor nodes.

questions.question: An inventory control system is an example of ___________?
questions.detailed_solution: An inventory control system is an example of On-demand reporting.

questions.question: Most problems in static WSN can be classified as -
questions.detailed_solution:
Most problems in static WSN can be classified as -
a. Area coverage
b. Point coverage
c. Barrier coverage

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of Stationary WSN?

options.text: Node failure may result in the partition of networks

questions.detailed_solution:
The disadvantages of Stationary WSN are -
a. Node failure may result in the partition of networks
b. Topology cannot be changed automatically

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "MANET-infrastructure less network of mobile devices connected wirelessly in WSN follow the self-HOP properties."
questions.detailed_solution:
MANET-infrastructure-less network of mobile devices connected
wirelessly in WSN follow the self-HOP properties.

questions.question: In Mobile WSN, the Data Mules

options.text: Collect the data from sensor nodes
Goes to the sink and delivers the collected data

questions.detailed_solution:
In Mobile WSN, the Data Mules
a. Collect the data from sensor nodes
b. Goes to the sink and delivers the collected data

questions.question: __________ senses different under the sea or water level parameters.
questions.detailed_solution: AUV senses different under the sea or water level parameters.

questions.question: Humans carry their devices and move around. Sensors embedded within the devices record readings. Sensory readings are then transmitted for processing. This paradigm of sensing is known as -
questions.detailed_solution:
Humans carry their devices and move around. Sensors embedded within
the devices record readings. Sensory readings are then transmitted for processing.
This paradigm of sensing is known as Human Centric Sensing.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Energy of Devices and Participant selection are two major problems in Human Centric Sensing."
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the challenges in human-centric sensing -
a. Energy of devices
b. Participant selection
c. Privacy of users

questions.question: Which of the following network topologies is used in UAV networks?
questions.detailed_solution: UAV networks use the mesh and star network topologies.

questions.question: The ______________ provides integrated services based on device collected data-sets.
questions.detailed_solution:
The M2M Application Platform provides integrated services based on
device collected data-sets.

questions.question: When different processing logic are applied to the same IoT networked devices or applications, it gives rise to?
questions.detailed_solution:
When different processing logic are applied to the same IoT networked devices or
applications, it gives rise to Semantic conflicts.
questions.reference: See lecture 21 (Interoperability in Internet of Things) @ 03:41.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Interoperability is a characteristic of a product or system."
questions.detailed_solution: Interoperability is a characteristic of a product or system.

questions.question: Interoperability is important in the context of IoT so that -
questions.detailed_solution:
Interoperability is important in the context of IoT so that -
a. Physical objects can interact
b. Any device can communicate with any other device
c. Networks integrate seamlessly

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false Statement: "Use of different programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, JAVA, and others is an example of heterogeneity in IoT. This brings in the need for interoperability."
questions.detailed_solution:
Use of different programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, JAVA, and others
is an example of heterogeneity in IoT. This brings in the need for interoperability

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "The interoperability between devices and device users in terms of message formats is called Syntactic Interoperability."
questions.detailed_solution:
The interoperability between devices and device users in terms of
message formats is called Syntactic Interoperability.

questions.question: What is the full form of UMB in IoT interoperability?
questions.detailed_solution: UMB stands for Universal Middleware Bridge.

questions.question: Which of the following pins is NULL in the DHT-11 sensor?
questions.detailed_solution: Pin 3 is NULL in the DHT-11 sensor.

questions.question: Arduino MEGA microcontroller board is also known as?
questions.detailed_solution: Arduino MEGA microcontroller board is also known as MEGA 2560.

questions.question: A ___________ provides high-precision rotary motion of 0 to 180 degrees.
questions.detailed_solution: A Servo Motor provides high-precision rotary motion of o to 180 degrees.

questions.question: What does the following code snippet do in interfacing a servo motor with the Arduino MEGA board?

options.text: Declares pin for connecting servo motor
Declares pin for providing power to MEGA board
Declares pin for Ground supply for servo motor

text: Declares pin for connecting servo motor

questions.detailed_solution: int servoPin = 12; Declares pin for connecting servo motor.
questions.answer_display: Declares pin for connecting servo motor

questions.question: What does the delay(1000); line do mean in an Arduino sketch?

options.text: It introduces a delay of 1 second
It introduces a delay of 10 seconds
It introduces a delay of 100 milliseconds

text: It introduces a delay of 1 second

questions.detailed_solution: The line delay(1000); in an Arduino sketch introduces a delay of 1 second.
questions.answer_display: It introduces a delay of 1 second

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Additional hardware is required to load a program into the Arduino Uno board using a computer."
questions.detailed_solution:
No additional hardware is required to load a program into the Arduino Uno
board using a computer.

questions.question: Arduino Uno board has ______ number of PWM pins?
questions.detailed_solution: Arduino Uno board has 6 number of PWM pins.

questions.question: Consider the following Arduino sketch.
questions.code_block: What must be inserted in the place of '??' within the second pinMode() function in void setup()?
questions.detailed_solution: Since pin 'g' is writing digital values, it is required to set in OUTPUT mode.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "The "Verify" option in the Arduino IDE checks the code for compilation errors."
questions.detailed_solution: The "Verify" option in the Arduino IDE checks the code for compilation errors.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Python is popular for embedded application development as it is a very lightweight programming language."
questions.detailed_solution:
Python is popular for embedded application development as it is a very
lightweight programming language.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 1:22)

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Python does not support strict rules for syntax."
questions.detailed_solution: Python does not support strict rules for syntax.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 03:38)

questions.question: _________ is a software that is used to write codes, integrate several modules and libraries.
questions.detailed_solution:
IDE is a software that is used to write codes, integrate several modules and
libraries.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 05:02).

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "To indicate different blocks of code, Python follows rigid indentation."
questions.detailed_solution: To indicate different blocks of code, Python follows rigid indentation.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 7::29).

questions.question: What is the output of the following line of code in Python?
questions.detailed_solution:
The output of the following line of code in Python -
>>> print "Hi, Welcome to python!"
Output: Hi, Welcome to python!
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 07:31)

questions.question: Which of the following is a mode to open a file in Python?
questions.detailed_solution:
The four basic modes to open a file in python are -
a. Read mode
b. Write mode
c. Append mode
d. Both read and write mode
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 03:00)

questions.question: State whether the following command to install the PIL library is correct or not. sudo pip get pillow
questions.detailed_solution: The command to install the PIL library is sudo pip install pillow.

questions.question: How many data types are these in Python?
questions.detailed_solution: There are 5 data types in Python.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @9:40).

questions.code_block:
if(condition):
statement 1
statement2
elif(condition)
statement 1
statement 2
else
statement 1
statement 2
questions.detailed_solution: Invalid as the colons are missing in elif and else statements.

questions.question: Can we configure Raspberry Pi as a Web Server?
questions.detailed_solution: We can configure Raspberry Pi as a Web Server.

questions.question: What is the frequency of the Raspberry Pi Zero CPU?
questions.detailed_solution: The frequency of the Raspberry Pi Zero CPU is 1 GHz.

questions.detailed_solution: It installs the Raspberry Pi GPIO library.

questions.detailed_solution: Ctrl + O exits the nano editor.

questions.question: Adafruit provides a library to work with DHT22 Sensor.
questions.detailed_solution:
Adafruit provides a library to work with DHT22 Sensor. (Please refer to

questions.question: Which python library is used for plotting the data in 2D.
questions.detailed_solution:
Matplotlib is a python library used for plotting the data in 2D. (Please
refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- III @ 12:13)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. __________ creates a two-way communication between two nodes in a network.
questions.detailed_solution:
Socket Programming creates a two-way communication between two
nodes in a network.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- II @ 6:02).

questions.question: What are the two main challenges of SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Rule placement and Controller placement are the two main challenges
of SDN.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Software Defined Networking- Part- I @ 16:49)

questions.question: IoT cannot control the network remotely.
questions.detailed_solution: IoT can control the network remotely.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- II @ 1:53)

questions.question: Which of the following is a part of data processing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Splitting and Filtering are both types of data processing. (Please refer to
lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- III @ 7:20)

questions.question: What is introduced if a controller is down in SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Backup controller is introduced if a controller is down in SDN. (Please
refer to lecture Software Defined Networking- Part- II @ 8:29 )

questions.question: Ion() is used to add a subplot in the data plotting in python.
questions.detailed_solution:
subplot() is used to add a subplot in the data plotting in python. (Please
refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- III @ 23:28).

questions.question: Matplotlib cannot plot in 2D.
questions.detailed_solution: Matplotlib can plot in 2D.

questions.question: How many rules are deleted from the switch at hard timeout?
questions.detailed_solution: All rules are deleted from the switch at hard timeout.

questions.question: Are controllers able to handle all incoming requests from switches?
questions.detailed_solution:
Controllers must be able to handle all incoming requests from
switches.

questions.question: Are Indigo and LINC Open source?
questions.detailed_solution: Indigo and LINC are both open source.

questions.question: Which of the following forward the sensed data based on the ID of the source node?
questions.detailed_solution:
ID-centric data forwarding forward the sensed data based on the ID
of the source node.

questions.detailed_solution:
A controller can handle 200 requests in a second through a single

questions.detailed_solution:
Switches that forward the traffic in a distributed manner do not have a

questions.question: Which of the following abstracts the physical resources from the network services?
questions.detailed_solution:
Network Virtualization abstracts the physical resources from the network
services

questions.question: In which of these, exact match rules are useful?
questions.detailed_solution: In Elephant Flow, exact match rules are useful.

questions.question: Which of these takes last k-th location instances to predict the next location?
questions.detailed_solution:
Order-k Markov predictor takes last k-th location instances to
predict the next location.

questions.question: Which of the following is an important issue due to the dynamic nature of the IoT network?
questions.detailed_solution:
Load Balancing is an important issue due to the dynamic
nature of the IoT network.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ___________ refers to heterogeneous computing nodes distributed over a wide area to perform very large tasks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Grid Computing refers to heterogeneous computing nodes
distributed over a wide area to perform very large tasks.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of service model in cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
All these are a type of service model in cloud computing. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about SaaS? Statement I: Applications are available as 'services'. Statement II: End-users do not have the control of the cloud infrastructure. Statement III: Services can be accessed by various types of client devices.
questions.detailed_solution:
All the three statements are correct for the SaaS. Applications
are available as 'services'. End-users do not have the control of the cloud
infrastructure. Services can be accessed by various types of client devices.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ means independent of device or location.
questions.detailed_solution:
Ubiquitous means independent of device or location. (Please
refer

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ provides the platform which allows developers to create applications which can be offered as a service via the Internet.
questions.detailed_solution:
Platform-as-a-Service provides the platform which allows
developers to create applications which can be offered as a service via the
Internet.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. 'Dynamic scaling of the required infrastructure resources' is a feature of which of the essential characteristic of cloud computing.
questions.detailed_solution:
'Dynamic scaling of the required infrastructure resources' is a
feature of scalability characteristics of cloud computing.

questions.question: Software-as-a-Service does not support a fully pay-as-you-go model.
questions.detailed_solution: Software-as-a-Service supports a fully pay-as-you-go model.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the Host Level Security at PaaS and SaaS level?

options.text: Hide host OS from the end users
Security responsibilities are transformed to Cloud service providers

questions.detailed_solution: Both a and b.

questions.question: Which of the following is an objective of Data Security?
questions.detailed_solution:
The three objectives of Data Security are Confidentiality, integrity
and availability.

questions.question: CloudSim provides a cloud environment.
questions.detailed_solution: CloudSim provides a cloud environment..

questions.question: Which of the following is true about GreenCloud?

options.text: GreenCloud cannot monitor the energy consumption of servers, switches, etc
GreenCloud was developed as an extension of GO

questions.detailed_solution:
GreenCloud monitors the energy consumption of servers,
switches, etc. It was developed as an extension of NS2.
questions.reference: (Please refer Lecture 40@16:25) Introduction to Internet of Things

questions.question: Which of the following can be used to install OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution: devstack can be used to install OpenStack.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the advantages of cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
The advantages of cloud computing include Elasticity, Pay-per-use and Self-
Service.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the limitations of WSNs. Statement I: Procurement Statement II: Deployment Statement III: Maintenance
questions.detailed_solution: The limitations of WSNs are Procurement, Deployment and Maintenance.

questions.question: Which of the following plays a role in the sensor-cloud architecture?
questions.detailed_solution:
End users, sensor-owner, sensor-cloud service provider are the actors in
sensor-cloud.

questions.question: The managerial role is played by __________ in sensor-cloud architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor-Cloud Service Provider plays the managerial role in
sensor-cloud architecture.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the components of the work-flow of the sensor-cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
Virtual Sensor Manager, Virtual Sensor Controller and Resource
Manager are all of the components of the work-flow of the sensor-cloud.

questions.question: Who coined the term Fog computing?
questions.detailed_solution: CISCO coined the term Fog computing.

questions.question: What of the following is responsible for allocation and deallocation of the resources?
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor resource pool is responsible for allocation and deallocation of the
resources

questions.question: The sensor owner can only deploy homogeneous physical sensor nodes in the sensor cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensor owner can deploy homogeneous and heterogeneous physical
sensor nodes in the sensor cloud.

questions.question: What is the function of Internal Cache?

options.text: Takes decision whether the data should be provided directly to the end user

questions.detailed_solution:
Internal Cache handles requests from end-user and also takes decision
whether the data should be provided directly to the end user

questions.question: What is the other term for fog computing?
questions.detailed_solution: The other term for fog computing is Fogging.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Fog computing is an intermediate layer between __________ and _________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog computing is an intermediate layer between Cloud and
devices.

questions.question: Bandwidth refers to the bit-rate of data during analysis.
questions.detailed_solution: Bandwidth refers to the bit-rate of data during transmission.

questions.question: Which of the following is used by the fog applications to increase the speed of service accessibility?
questions.detailed_solution:
Accelerators increase the speed of service accessibility. (Please
refer to Page 260, Chapter 11, Introduction to IoT. S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A.
Roy, 2020. Cambridge University Press.)
_________________________________________________________________________

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The concept of ________ enables physical hardware to be shared among multiple entities.
questions.detailed_solution:
The concept of hardware virtualization enables physical hardware
to be shared among multiple entities. (Please refer to Page 262, Chapter 11,
Introduction to IoT. S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Cambridge
University Press.)
___________________________________________________________________

questions.question: Smart city is an _____________ system.
questions.detailed_solution: Smart city is an urban system.

questions.question: Which of the following is an area of focus in smart parking lots?

options.text: Detection of slots in parking lots

questions.detailed_solution:
Auto-routing of vehicles, auto-charging of vehicles and
detection of slots in parking lots.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that are true for smart health. Statement I: Smart health is low-cost and portable. Statement II: Smart health allows remote check-ups and diagnosis. Statement III: Smart health is effortless health monitoring.
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart health is low-cost, portable and effortless health
monitoring. Smart health allows remote check-ups and diagnosis.

questions.question: What refers to combining information from multiple sensor sources?
questions.detailed_solution:
Multi-sensor data fusion combines information from multiple
sensor sources.

questions.question: The ensemble of all the decisions occur at the __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The ensemble of all the decisions occur at the
Decision level.

questions.question: The smart parking reduces traffic __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The smart parking reduces traffic congestion. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct full form of DLNA Standards for HAN?
questions.detailed_solution:
DLNA stands for Digital Living Network Alliance
standards.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of sensing?
questions.detailed_solution: Stationary and Mobile are two types of sensing.

questions.question: Smart home infrastructure cannot include wireless devices.
questions.detailed_solution: Smart home infrastructure can include wireless/wired devices.

questions.question: Which of the following is a layer of Zigbee ?
questions.detailed_solution:
The layers of Zigbee are Physical layer, MAC Layer, Network
Layer are all the layers of Zigbee.

questions.question: Which of the following is a phase of ICV development?
questions.detailed_solution:
The phases of ICV development: Phase 1: Based on
2G, Phase 2: Based on 4G LTE, Phase 3: Vehicles connected to cloud

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of V2X?
questions.detailed_solution: V2X follows a distributed architecture.

questions.question: The vehicles cannot be mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of VANET.
questions.detailed_solution:
The vehicles are mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of
VANET.

questions.question: Which of the following is a domain of VANET?
questions.detailed_solution:
Both of the In-vehicle and Ad-hoc are the two
domains of VANETs.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ICV is based on ________.
questions.detailed_solution:
ICV is based on Dedicated Short Range
Communication.

questions.question: What are some of the other names of the smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grids are also known as Electricity with a brain, Energy
internet, and Electronet.

questions.question: Smart Grid follows which type of flow of energy?
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grid follows bidirectional flow of energy.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit associated with smart grids for customers?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart Grids provide different pricing options for
customers, lower electricity rates and Improved security.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of smart grid related to power system efficiency?
questions.detailed_solution:
Power monitoring, Asset Management and
Distribution automation are all the properties of smart grid related
to power system efficiency.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In ___________, a home can have power from distributed resources.
questions.detailed_solution:
In islanding, a home can have power from distributed
resources.

questions.question: Which of the following aggregate the energy consumption or energy request of a certain geographical area?
questions.detailed_solution:
DAUs aggregate the energy consumption or energy
request of a certain geographical area.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about Dynamic System Attacks? Statement I: Replay attacks compromises sensors and monitors their outputs. Statement II: D-DIA can lead to a system collapse. Statement III: Covert attack is a closed loop version of replay attacks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Replay attacks compromises sensors and monitors
their outputs. D-DIA can lead to a system collapse. Covert attack is a
closed loop version of replay attacks.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is performed by compromising the availability of the grid component.
questions.detailed_solution:
Denial of service is performed by compromising the
availability of the grid component.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. IIoT is a network of ____________ .
questions.detailed_solution:
IIoT is a network of Physical objects, Systems and
Platforms.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is based on a wrap and re-use approach.
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT is based on the wrap and re-use approach.

questions.question: Mechanized production is a part of the 4th Industrial Revolution.
questions.detailed_solution:
Mechanized production is a part of the 1st Industrial
Revolution.

questions.question: Which of the following happened in the 3rd Revolution?
questions.detailed_solution:
Internet evolution and automation happened in the
3rd Revolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to improve the interoperability of different systems?
questions.detailed_solution:
Standardization improves the interoperability of
different systems.

questions.question: Big data is characterized by 7 Vs.
questions.detailed_solution: Big data is characterized by 7 Vs.

questions.question: What is Hadoop used in?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hadoop is used for distributed processing of large
datasets across Large clusters of computers

questions.question: Qualitative analysis does not deal with the analysis of data that is categorical in nature.
questions.detailed_solution:
Qualitative analysis deals with the analysis of data that is
categorical in nature.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Examples of dispersion measures include _______ and ______.
questions.detailed_solution: Examples of dispersion measures include Range and Variance

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the key variables of determining statistical significance. Statement I: Range size Statement II: Effect size Statement III: Sample size
questions.detailed_solution:
The key variables of determining statistical significance include effect size
and sample size

questions.question: The different objectives of AgriSens includes -
questions.detailed_solution:
The different objectives of AgriSens includes More yields with less water,
Save limited water resources and Automatic irrigation.

questions.question: The two most relevant sensors directly used in agriculture are __________.

options.text: Soil moisture and water level sensor
ECG sensor and water level sensor

text: Soil moisture and water level sensor

questions.detailed_solution:
Soil moisture and water level sensors are the necessary sensors
generally used in agriculture.
questions.answer_display: Soil moisture and water level sensor

questions.question: The integrated design of the remote server includes______________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The integrated design of the remote server includes repository data
server, web server and multi-users server.

questions.question: Select the correct order of the component layers present in the IoT healthcare.

options.text: Sensing layer, cloud platform layer, aggregated layer, processing layer
Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer
Aggregated layer, sensing layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer
Sensing layer, processing layer, aggregated layer, cloud platform layer

text: Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer

questions.detailed_solution:
The sensing layer senses data and transmit it o the aggregation layer where
the data are aggregated. The aggregated layer further transfers the data to the processing layer
the data are processed and final sent to the cloud platform.
questions.answer_display: Sensing layer, aggregated layer, processing layer, cloud platform layer

questions.question: Which of the following networks is used in AmbuSens?
questions.detailed_solution:
In AmbuSens, the network used is Wireless Body Area Network. (Please
refer

questions.question: Wireless IoT driven solutions for remote healthcare facility provisioning brings healthcare to patients than bringing patients to healthcare.
questions.detailed_solution:
In IoT health care, wireless IoT driven solutions bring healthcare to patients
than bringing patients to healthcare.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The statistical data analysis tools can be applied on_____________.
questions.detailed_solution: The statistical data analysis tools can be applied on sensor data.

questions.question: Which of the following sensors are present in smartphones handheld devices are used for activity monitoring?
questions.detailed_solution:
Accelerometer and Gyroscope are used for activity
monitoring.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary disadvantage of using camera-based activity tracking?
questions.detailed_solution: Camera based activity tracking is very much process intensive.

questions.question: By performing continuous monitoring of a person's activity, it is not possible to observe his/her behavior or to identify any repetitive pattern in his/her day-to-day activity.
questions.detailed_solution:
Continuous monitoring of activity results in daily observations
of human behavior and repetitive patterns in their activity.

questions.question: Which of the following are the inbuilt sensors that are present in high end smartphones?
questions.detailed_solution:
High end smartphones are likely to have accelerometer,
compass, and gyroscope.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be used for ________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be
used for detecting sudden fall of a person.

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
